raw_consumer_statement:
I was charged approximately {$1200.00} for hotel accommodation booked through XXXX The stay was never used. 

I cancelled the reservation before the stay due to documented safety concerns following multiple significant earthquakes in XXXX within a short period, including strong earthquakes and subsequent tremors felt in the XXXX metropolitan area. My cancellation was based on safety considerations, not a discretionary change of plans. 

I submitted a formal billing dispute to Citibank, explaining that the service was never used and providing context regarding the safety-related reason for cancellation. I also contacted the merchant and XXXX in good faith. Both declined to provide a refund and relied solely on the cancellation policy without addressing the safety concerns. 

During the dispute process, Citibank did not provide a clear explanation of how the dispute was investigated, what evidence was reviewed, or how it determined the charge was valid despite the service not being used. Citibank ultimately resolved the dispute in the merchants favor without addressing the substance of my claim or explaining how my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act were applied. 

I am submitting this complaint because I believe Citibank failed to conduct a reasonable and transparent investigation of my billing dispute and failed to adequately explain its decision, as required under applicable consumer protection laws. I am seeking correction of the disputed charge and proper handling of my billing dispute.
